revisedPrompt:
Generate exactly one PNG image for a game-development pixel-art terrain-tile asset.

STRICT OUTPUT CONTRACT:
- One single PNG image only.
- Canvas/sheet size: exactly 64x64 pixels.
- Pixel art sprite sheet: 4 rows x 4 columns.
- Frame/cell size: exactly 16x16 pixels.
- Total frames: 16.
- No visible grid lines, no borders, no tile IDs, no labels, no text, no watermark, no UI.
- Every frame stays fully inside its own 16x16 cell, same scale and perspective across all cells.
- Solid #FF00FF magenta background fills all empty space only.
- Crisp low-resolution pixels, no antialiasing blur, no painterly smoothing.
- 16-bit SNES-style, bright cheerful mythical-Japan satoyama palette, top-left light source, fine readable pixel clusters, dark one-pixel outline where useful.

SUBJECT:
Top-down-OBLIQUE mossy stone staircase tileset descending toward the viewer at about 35 degrees. The staircase must be tileable vertically into a continuous stair run. Use mossy grey fieldstone steps, deep matcha grass, soft warm sunlight. Step risers must be clearly readable and dark; tread tops lighter. Low stone side walls / parapets on both left and right sides of the stair run. Side walls are short, about half-step height, made of cut stone, with cap stones in a slightly warmer pale grey kasaishi-like color. Side wall height must remain consistent relative to the step treads in every row. The stone steps are flat-faced mossy fieldstones like ishigaki wall stones.

STRICT 4x4 LAYOUT, left to right, top to bottom:
Row 1, stair top where staircase meets plateau:
- Cell 0: top landing left side wall cap, plateau grass/stone edge blending into stair start.
- Cell 1: top landing center, step meets flat plateau ground.
- Cell 2: top landing center variation with subtle moss/grass overlap.
- Cell 3: top landing right side wall cap, plateau grass/stone edge blending into stair start.

Row 2, upper middle of stair run:
- Cell 4: left side wall plus step riser.
- Cell 5: step center with clear dark riser and lighter tread.
- Cell 6: step center variation with moss on fieldstones.
- Cell 7: right side wall plus step riser.

Row 3, lower middle of stair run:
- Cell 8: left side wall plus step.
- Cell 9: step center with clear dark riser and lighter tread.
- Cell 10: step center variation with chipped stones and small moss clusters.
- Cell 11: right side wall plus step.

Row 4, stair bottom where staircase meets lower ground:
- Cell 12: bottom footing left with side wall base and lower grass.
- Cell 13: bottom landing center, steps meeting lower matcha grass.
- Cell 14: bottom landing center variation with warm sunlight and soft grass overlap.
- Cell 15: bottom footing right with side wall base and lower grass.

QUALITY RULES:
- Tilemap should not read as a checkerboard when assembled; vary texture, edge breakup, moss, chipped stones, and lighting while keeping the set cohesive.
- Make all transition edges blend naturally into grass and stone, avoiding hard rectangular bands.
- World-map material sensibility: readable adventure geography at tile scale, not close-up village detail.
- Keep each 16x16 tile filled edge-to-edge with usable terrain content, except for any truly empty corner space which must be solid #FF00FF.
- No straight vertical or horizontal strips that look artificial; stair edges and walls should have subtle broken stone silhouettes while remaining tileable.

AVOID COMPLETELY:
Mario, Nintendo, Zelda, Final Fantasy, Pokemon, Dragon Quest, Secret of Mana, Okami, Sakuna characters, logos or likeness; no samurai armor; no kabuto helmet; no ninja outfit; no two-sword daisho; no gun; no modern objects; no horror mood; no dark gloomy palette; no bird or companion creature; no letter emblems; no modern text; no watermark; no UI.
